note: Resolves full-script research blockers and narrows biological-risk, information-integrity, labour and AI Act wording to what authoritative sources support. The first two-minute proof narration is unchanged.

source_additions.publisher: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence
source_additions.title: The 2025 AI Index Report — Economy
source_additions.limitation: Investment levels show the scale of competition; they do not directly prove that every investor rewards speed over caution.

source_additions.publisher: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence
source_additions.title: The 2025 AI Index Report — Technical Performance
source_additions.limitation: Benchmark proximity is evidence of a crowded frontier, not a complete measure of commercial leadership or safety.

source_additions.title: AI Foundation Models: Update paper
visual_evidence_candidates: Critical inputs and partnerships risk summary
source_additions.limitation: The report identifies competition risks and market structures; it does not establish that concentration is inevitable.

source_additions.title: Cloud services market investigation — final decision
source_additions.limitation: This investigation concerns cloud infrastructure services broadly, not only frontier AI training.

source_additions.publisher: International Scientific Report on the Safety of Advanced AI / UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
source_additions.title: International AI Safety Report 2025
source_additions.limitation: The report finds strong evidence that AI can generate persuasive content cheaply at scale, but limited evidence and no scientific consensus on broad societal or electoral effects.

claim_additions.narration_excerpt: AI makes convincing synthetic content cheaper to produce at scale, while the broader societal effects remain uncertain.
evidence_requirements: Show the evidence chain from content generation to distribution to possible societal effect.
State that evidence becomes weaker at later stages and that election-level effects are not established.

claim_overrides.narration_excerpt: Competition is visible in the money and in how narrow the frontier has become.
evidence_requirements: Show 2024 generative-AI investment and frontier-performance convergence with exact source/date.
Do not state that investment figures prove every actor prioritizes speed over safety.

claim_overrides.narration_excerpt: Removed: the specific late-2025 internal-emergency anecdote lacked sufficiently authoritative sourcing.
evidence_requirements: Do not restore this anecdote without a direct, attributable primary source.
claim_overrides.recommended_rewrite: Use verified investment and frontier-convergence evidence instead of a reported internal emergency.

claim_overrides.narration_excerpt: Anthropic activated additional ASL-3 safeguards as a precaution while saying it had not definitively determined that its capability threshold had been crossed.
evidence_requirements: Show the precautionary and provisional nature of the ASL-3 activation.
Do not generalize one laboratory's internal testing into an industry-wide conclusion.

claim_overrides.narration_excerpt: The evidence measures exposure more reliably than it predicts unemployment.
evidence_requirements: Show the ILO warning that exposure is not a direct job-loss forecast.

claim_overrides.narration_excerpt: Frontier AI depends on costly compute, cloud infrastructure, data and capital.
evidence_requirements: Show the CMA's identified risks around critical inputs, partnerships and routes to market.
State that the cloud investigation is broader than frontier AI.

claim_overrides.narration_excerpt: The AI Act separates high-risk system duties from obligations for general-purpose model providers, with additional evaluation, incident-reporting and cybersecurity duties for systemic-risk models.
evidence_requirements: Separate AI-system and GPAI-model obligations visually and verbally.
Show the 10^25 FLOP presumption as rebuttable and updateable, not an absolute definition.
